description: Fix resource leaks and dead code in the MCP server: orphaned npm child processes on timeout, unreachable error logging in deleteExecFile, and invisible exec file cleanup failures.

userStories.title: Kill child process on runNpmGlobal timeout
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want timed-out npm subprocesses to be killed so that orphaned processes don't accumulate and consume system resources.
acceptanceCriteria: When the 60s timeout fires in runNpmGlobal, the spawned npm child process is killed before the timeout error is thrown
spawnAsync returns or exposes a kill handle so the caller can terminate the child
The existing behavior for non-timeout cases (successful completion and non-zero exit) is unchanged
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts, lines 63-118.

spawnAsync (line 64) creates a child via spawn() but only returns a Promise<{exitCode, stdout, stderr}> — the ChildProcess handle is encapsulated and inaccessible. When the timeout wins Promise.race at line 101, the child process keeps running as an orphan.

Fix approach: make spawnAsync return an object with both a result promise and a kill function (or return the ChildProcess alongside the promise). In runNpmGlobal, call kill() in the timeout path before re-throwing. The finally block already clears the timer — add the kill there or in the timeout callback.

Be careful: spawnAsync is also used by other callers in the same file (searchNpmRegistry at ~line 153, installPlugin, uninstallPlugin). Ensure the interface change doesn't break those call sites.

userStories.title: Fix deleteExecFile dead code and error logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I want deleteExecFile to actually log failures so that exec file cleanup issues are diagnosable rather than silently swallowed.
acceptanceCriteria: deleteExecFile logs a warning via log.warn when unlink fails
The .catch(() => {}) that swallows the unlink error is removed
The outer try-catch correctly handles the unlink rejection

userStories.title: Await exec file deletion in execute-script handler
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want exec file deletion errors to be logged so that stale temporary files from execute-script don't accumulate silently on long-running servers.
acceptanceCriteria: execute-script handler awaits deleteExecFile in its finally block instead of using void fire-and-forget
deleteExecFile errors do not propagate to the caller (the finally block catches or the function itself is fire-and-forget safe)
The handler still returns the dispatch result, not the deletion result
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/execute-script.ts, line 50.

Current code uses `void deleteExecFile(filename)` in the finally block, making deletion fire-and-forget. Combined with US-002's fix (deleteExecFile will now actually log errors), this story ensures the await happens so the log.warn in deleteExecFile has a chance to execute before the function context is garbage collected.

Since deleteExecFile already wraps unlink in try-catch (after US-002's fix), awaiting it won't throw. The finally block should use `await deleteExecFile(filename)` instead of `void deleteExecFile(filename)`.

This story depends on US-002 being completed first (same PRD, sequential execution).
